heroHeadline: PLA Re-Escalates to 15 Sorties on Navy 77th Anniversary — 14 of 15 Enter SW ADIZ; 21 Days to Trump-Xi Summit
heroSubtitle: Taiwan's MND detected 15 PLA aircraft sorties and 5 PLAN vessels on April 23, 2026 — a significant re-escalation from the 6-sortie pullback of April 22, coinciding precisely with the PLA Navy's 77th founding anniversary (April 23, 1949). Of the 15 sorties, 14 entered Taiwan's southwestern ADIZ — a concentrated southwestern penetration rather than the multi-sector pattern of the April 21 24-sortie surge. The April 23 spike disrupts the expected post-peak de-escalation arc (Liaoning transit Apr 20 → 24-sortie surge Apr 21 → 6-sortie pullback Apr 22 → 15-sortie anniversary re-escalation Apr 23), confirming PLA is running a sustained pre-summit pressure campaign with no stable de-escalation floor. Simultaneously, the PLA Navy conducted record-scale 77th anniversary open-ship events: 40+ active-duty vessels opened to the public in 10+ cities (Dalian, Qingdao, Shanghai, Ningbo, Xiamen, Guangzhou, Sanya) — the largest such event in PLA Navy history — projecting a dual message of soft power and military readiness. With 21 days to the Trump-Xi summit in Beijing (May 14-15), Beijing continues its dual-track strategy: anniversary symbolism for domestic audiences alongside sustained ADIZ pressure against Taiwan's DPP government. The $14B PAC-3/NASAMS arms package remains held pending the summit; Taiwan's NT$1.25T special defense budget remains deadlocked in the legislature with US Indo-Pacific Command urging passage.
footerNote: Data sourced from Taiwan MND, US DoD, DSCA, CSIS ChinaPower, Global Taiwan Institute, AIT, Reuters, AP, Bloomberg, NPR, Taipei Times, Focus Taiwan, Nikkei Asia, Japan Times, ANI News, Tribune India, Vision Times, South China Morning Post, Taiwan News, Asia Times, The Asia Cable, GlobalSecurity.org, Xinhua, CGTN, The Diplomat, Washington Times, Global Times, People's Daily Online, Al Jazeera, The Manila Times, CNBC, MarineLink, Stars and Stripes, Japan News Pravda, US News & World Report, Free Malaysia Today, and institutional analyses. All claims reflect multi-pole sourcing across Western, East Asian, and international media.
